name: Italian Flat Bread (Focaccia) -Hand
recipeIngredient: 2 1/4 teaspoons Active Dry Yeast or Quick-Rise Yeast
recipeInstructions:
This outstanding Focaccia recipe from Red Star Yeast was a hit with seasoned pinto beans. In a large mixing bowl, combine 1 1/2 cups flour, yeast, 1 tablespoon sugar, and 1 teaspoon salt. Mix well with warm water and olive oil until the dough is moistened. Blend at low speed for about 3 minutes.
Gradually stir in enough remaining flour to form a firm dough using your hands. Knead on a floured surface for 5-8 minutes, adding more flour as needed. Place the dough in a greased bowl, turning to coat the top. Cover and let rise in a warm place for about 40 minutes (20 minutes for Quick-Rise Yeast).
Prepare the onion topping: In a small skillet, sauté onions in butter until soft. Stir in 1/3 teaspoon sugar and 1/8 teaspoon salt.
Punch down the dough, shape it into a ball on a lightly floured surface, and place on a greased cookie sheet. Flatten to a 10-inch circle. Cut a circle about 1 inch from the edge with a table knife, almost reaching the cookie sheet. Prick the center with a fork.
Spread the onion topping over the pricked dough. Cover and let rise in a warm place for another 30 minutes (15 minutes for Quick-Rise Yeast). Bake at 375°F until golden brown for about 25-30 minutes. For variation, omit the onion topping and cut a criss-cross design with a sharp knife instead. Brush with an egg glaze made from 1 egg yolk mixed with 1 tablespoon milk or water, and sprinkle with sesame seeds if desired.
